items.title: US Commerce Department confirms harsh new AI export rules, shoots down reports over the return of Biden-era AI Diffusion rule — DoC to formalize a new approach to strategic AI accelerator export controls
items.description: The U.S. Commerce confirms work on new export regulations for AI accelerators, but implies they will not resemble the ill-fated AI Diffusion Rule.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The U.S. Department of Commerce has issued a statement confirming that the U.S. government is looking to change AI hardware export rules, but provided little by way of detail. The officials confirmed that the U.S. DoC is looking to formalize an approach under which buyers of large quantities of AI accelerators must invest in U.S. AI infrastructure to obtain their hardware.Go deeper with TH Premium: Taiwan, trade, and tariffs (Image credit: tsmc)China's latest round of rare-earth export controls explainedAnalyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rulesU.S. government plans tariff exemptions for TSMCNvidia wants China's market share to secure the future of CUDA in the region"The Commerce Department is committed to promoting secure exports of the American tech stack," a statement by the U.S. DoC reads. "We successfully advanced exports through our historic Middle East agreements, and there are ongoing internal government discussions about formalizing that approach."The newly proposed export rules introduce a multi-level licensing structure tied to computing capacity. Smaller shipments of up to 1,000 Nvidia GB300 GPUs would undergo an expedited approval process; medium-scale deployments would need to secure pre-authorization from the U.S. Department of Commerce before applying for export license as well as operational transparency, disclosure of business activities, and potential on-site inspections by U.S. authorities; while planned large AI clusters set to use 200,000 of GB300 GPUs or more and operated by one entity in one country would require commitments to invest in U.S. AI infrastructure as part of the arrangement as well as intergovernmental talks with the U.S. to make national security assurances. You may like U.S. government preps sweeping export controls for Nvidia, AMD AI hardware Analyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rules U.S. posts official H200 and MI325X AI GPU export rules to China, but with plenty of caveats The terms attached to recent export licenses allowing Cerebras and Nvidia to supply hardware to the United Arab Emirates included a requirement that the Middle Eastern country invest one dollar in U.S. AI infrastructure for every dollar spent on its own domestic AI buildouts. If the same terms were to be attached to export licenses to other countries, then this would make hardware from AMD, Cerebras, Nvidia, and other suppliers of AI accelerators 2X more expensive for companies in these countries.The U.S. DoC shot down claims of a return to anything resembling the AI Diffusion Rule from the Joe Biden era: "Today there was reporting that we were returning to the AI diffusion rule," the statement reads. "We will not. It was burdensome, overreaching, and disastrous."While the AI Diffusion Rule was indeed very complicated, some approaches of the new rumored regulations would make them even more burdensome than the criticized export regime from early 2025.The AI Diffusion Rule divided countries into three tiers based on trust and risk levels. Tier 1 included the U.S. and 18 close allies like Australia, Canada, Japan, Taiwan, the U.K., and much of Western Europe, which faced minimal restrictions on AI exports. In Tier 2 countries, entities could import up to 1,700 Nvidia H100 GPUs (or equivalent) without a license, and these did not count toward national AI chip limits. Tier 3 was banned altogether.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMeanwhile, getting up to 320,000 Nvidia H100 GPUs required entities in Tier 2 countries to qualify as National Validated End Users (NVEUs) and meet strict security requirements. Unverified organizations in Tier 2 were allowed to buy up to 50,000 GPUs per country, though the limit was expandable to 100,000 if their government reached a formal agreement with the U.S. Under no circumstances did the AI Diffusion Rule mandate investments in U.S. AI infrastructure by purchasers of American AI hardware.If the new regulation is enacted, anything above 1,000 GB300 would be subject to pre-authorization before export licenses could be issued, which includes shipments to 18 allied nations. Getting 200,000 or more GB300 GPUs would essentially mandate investments in the U.S. AI buildouts. Then again, the reported rules are not final, and many of the requirements may be removed in the final versions of the proposed export regime. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 美国商务部敲定严格AI出口新规，澄清拜登时期扩散管制重返传闻——将正式确立战略AI加速器出口管控新框架
items.descriptionZh:
美国商务部近期发布声明，证实政府计划修改人工智能硬件出口规则，旨在将大规模采购美国AI加速器的行为与投资美国本土AI基础设施挂钩。该声明虽未提供完整细则，但披露了正在讨论中的多层级许可架构，这将显著改变包括英伟达、AMD、赛热（Cerebras）等公司AI芯片的全球销售条件。

**背景与政策动机**

美国商务部在声明中强调，其致力于推动“美国技术栈的安全出口”，并指出已在与中东达成的历史性协议中成功运用了相关方法，目前正进行内部政府讨论以正式确立该模式。其核心政策意图在于：在允许先进AI硬件出口的同时，确保美国在人工智能基础设施和生态系统发展上持续获益，并强化对高端算力流向的监管与安全控制。

**核心技术规则与层级结构**

拟议的新规以算力规模（具体以英伟达GB300 GPU数量为基准）为核心，建立了三层审批与约束机制：

1.  **小规模出货（≤1000颗GB300 GPU）**：适用简化快速审批流程，限制最宽松。
2.  **中规模部署**：在申请出口许可证前，需获得美国商务部的事前授权。此外，采购方需承诺运营透明度、披露商业活动，并可能接受美国当局的现场检查。
3.  **大规模集群（≥20万颗GB300 GPU，且由单一实体在同一国家运营）**：这是新规最具约束力的部分。采购方必须承诺对美国AI基础设施进行投资，作为交易安排的一部分。同时，还需通过两国政府间谈判提供国家安全保证。

**投资捆绑模式与成本影响**

新规的关键创新点在于引入“投资捆绑”条件。这一模式已在近期美国向阿拉伯联合酋长国发放的出口许可中得到应用，其条款要求阿联酋在自身AI建设上每花费1美元，就必须向美国AI基础设施投资1美元。若此模式推广至其他国家的出口许可，意味着相关国家企业购买AMD、赛热、英伟达等公司AI加速器的实际成本将翻倍。

**与旧规（AI扩散规则）的对比分析**

美国商务部明确否认新规是回归2025年初实施的、已被批评为“繁琐、过度且具有破坏性”的“AI扩散规则”。二者存在根本区别：

*   **AI扩散规则**：根据信任与风险水平将国家分为三个层级，对不同层级的芯片进口数量设限（如第二层级国家未经许可可进口最多1700颗英伟达H100 GPU）。它从未强制要求采购方投资美国AI基础设施。大规模采购（如最高32万颗H100）需通过“国家验证最终用户”（NVUE）严格安全审查。
*   **新拟议规则**：核心不在于国别分层，而在于采购规模。它将监管和投资义务直接与订单量挂钩。即使是美国18个亲密盟友（如澳大利亚、加拿大、日本、英国及西欧多国），只要采购量超过1000颗GB300，也将面临事前授权等限制；若达到20万颗或以上，则必须承诺对美投资。

**技术影响与应用场景展望**

若新规实施，将产生深远影响：
1.  **供应链与成本**：全球企业构建大规模AI计算集群的成本和复杂性将急剧上升，可能迫使部分需求转向本土或其他地区的替代解决方案。
2.  **产业生态**：通过强制资金回流，旨在强化美国本土在数据中心、算力网络等AI基础设施领域的领先地位，可能改变全球AI投资格局。
3.  **地缘技术竞争**：规则将商业采购与地缘政治承诺（政府间安全保证）捆绑，使高端AI硬件成为外交与安全政策的工具。
4.  **企业战略**：依赖美国顶级AI芯片的云服务商、大型科技公司及国家主导的AI项目，需重新评估其长期算力获取策略与供应链安全。

需要指出的是，目前报道的规则细节并非最终版本，许多要求在正式法规出台时可能被修改或删除。然而，其反映出的美国通过出口管制捆绑产业利益与战略目标的趋势已十分清晰。

items.title: Asus GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition review:  Silent running
items.description: Asus’ GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition is built with a single-minded focus on quiet operation. It takes the world’s second-fastest gaming GPU and reduces its noise levels to the absolute minimum thanks to an enormous heatsink and three cutting-edge Noctua fans. But its size, weight, high price, and polarizing design all make it a product for the Noctua faithful and quiet computing obsessives only.
items.content: Asus and Noctua have been collaborating on ultra-quiet graphics cards for some time now by pairing Noctua’s most advanced 120mm fans with massive custom heatsinks. That collaboration has continued in the Blackwell generation with the Asus RTX 5080 Noctua Edition, an absolutely ginormous air-cooled graphics card that promises no-compromises performance and temperatures alongside the lowest possible noise levels. That’s an exceedingly high bar to clear in one product. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Making air-cooled computer hardware quieter is simple enough, in theory. Improve thermal transfer by adding a vapor chamber or heat pipes to the base plate of a heatsink, increase its surface area by adding more and larger fins to the fin stack, and take advantage of the improved heat dissipation by slowing down the fans cooling said heatsink. Eventually, you get imperceptible noise levels. Easy enough, right?In practice, this recipe runs into all sorts of obstacles. Cases can only accept so large a heatsink without running into clearance issues. Sockets, slots, and PCBs can only take so much weight before they start to deform. The heatsink itself can only be so costly as part of the overall bill of materials. If you’re an engineer designing a typical graphics card for the typical PC, you have to balance all these concerns, and louder, faster-spinning fans on a smaller heatsink are typically one result of those tradeoffs.The Asus GeForce RTX 5080 Noctua Edition embraces an entirely different set of constraints. This graphics card is all about low noise levels, and the two companies have spared no effort or expense in making the quietest possible GPU air cooler out there, size and weight be damned.We’ve had the pleasure of reviewing some of Asus’s Noctua Edition cards in the past, and they’ve certainly provided both impressive noise levels and thermal performance. But this RTX 5080 marks the first time the duo has deployed three such fans on a Noctua Edition graphics card: in this case, NF-A12x25 G2s. As someone who got his start as a case and heatsink reviewer many moons ago, those fans immediately stand out as something different. $1,699.99 at ASUS $2,072.99 at Amazon Check Walmart (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The NF-A12x25 G2s boast a list of engineering refinements that would make an aeronautical engineer blush. Everything from the curvature of each blade to the ridges on the fan hub to the winglets at each blade tip is said to be optimized to improve the distribution and evenness of airflow with typical Noctua obsessiveness. The impellers (or rotors) themselves sit so close to the fan frame that trying to slide a sheet of printer paper between them will make the impeller move. Crazy stuff.To further refine the noise character of the card, Noctua has supplied Asus with two types of NF-A12x25 G2 fans, one of which runs slightly slower and the other of which runs slightly faster than the other. Noctua says this avoids “periodic humming or vibrations caused by beat frequencies.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The fans are paired with a custom heatsink that pairs a vapor-chamber baseplate with 11 heat pipes running through a 14.5” long fin stack, which is certainly plenty of metal. But not all of the card’s four-slot height is dedicated to fins and heat pipes. About two slots are occupied by the full-size NF-A12x25 G2 fans themselves. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We didn’t take our Noctua Edition apart because it’s a loaner, but we can see that the huge vapor chamber at the base of the heatsink covers both the GPU itself and the GDDR7 memory that rings it for a complete thermal solution. The VRM power phases and inductors are also joined to the fin stack with their own metal contact plate and thermal pads. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)All told, this is one of the largest and heaviest air-cooled graphics cards I’ve ever handled, weighing in at a whopping 5.9 lb (2.7 kg). It absolutely dwarfs the RTX 5080 Founders Edition. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)For all its focus on quiet operation, the design of the RTX 5080 Noctua Edition is inescapably polarizing. The plastic fan shroud and metal backplate are all finished in a muted brown color with a subtle sparkle that only reveals itself under direct lighting. The NF-A12x25 G2 fans themselves are the brown-and-tan models that Noctua obsessives will love and the uninitiated may hate. I think it’s great, but other Tom’s Hardware staffers can’t stand it. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The Noctua faithful will find plenty of subtle nods to its brand on this 5080, and these touches are all done with the same subtlety and attention to detail typical of other Noctua products. Metallic accents on the face of the card suggest wings or eyebrows, and the flow-through cutout on the backplate borrows one half of Noctua’s owl logo. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The painted stripes on the backplate that suggest stampings or embossing are so well done that they made me do a double-take to ensure they weren’t actually part of the metal. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)All told, this card will look fan-tastic in a Noctua-themed build, but many will be left wishing for a Chromax model that’s dressed entirely in black for better coordination in the average PC. Maybe a future Noctua Edition can cover both bases, but for now, brown is all you get.Let's take a look at performance, power, and thermals on the following pages.SpecsSwipe to scroll horizontallyHeader Cell - Column 0 RTX 5080 Founders EditionAsus RTX 5080 Noctua EditionGPUNvidia GB203Nvidia GB203SMs8484CUDA Cores1075210752Boost Clock (MHz)26172700VRAM TypeGDDR7GDDR7VRAM Clock17501750VRAM Capacity (GB)1616Memory Bandwidth (GB/s)960960Peak FP32 TFLOPS (Boost)56.358.1TGP (Watts)360360Power connectors1x 12V-2x61x 12V-2x6Recommended PSU (Watts)850850Dimensions (LWD)12" x 4.8" x 1.6" (30.5 x 12.2 x 4 cm)15" x 5.3" x 3.2" (38.2 x 13.6 x 8.1 cm)Weight3.6 lb (1.6 kg)5.9 lb (2.67 kg)MSRP$999.99$1,699.99MORE: Best Graphics CardsMORE: GPU Benchmarks and HierarchyMORE: All Graphics Content
items.author: Jeffrey Kampman
items.titleZh: 华硕GeForce RTX 5080猫头鹰版评测：静默运行
items.descriptionZh:
华硕（Asus）与著名散热厂商猫头鹰（Noctua）长期合作，致力于打造极致静音的高性能显卡。双方的合作现已延续至Blackwell世代，推出了华硕RTX 5080猫头鹰版。这款显卡采用巨型风冷散热方案，旨在提供无妥协的性能与温度控制，同时实现尽可能低的噪音水平，这是一项极具挑战性的目标。

理论上，降低风冷硬件噪音的方法很直接：通过增加均热板或热管来增强导热效率，通过扩大鳍片面积来提升散热表面积，从而允许风扇以更低转速运行，最终达到几乎无感的噪音水平。然而，在实际产品设计中，工程师们面临诸多限制：机箱兼容性、插槽与PCB的承重能力，以及成本控制。因此，市面上常见的显卡往往需要在散热规模与风扇转速之间做出权衡，其结果通常是更小的散热器搭配更高转速、更大噪音的风扇。

华硕GeForce RTX 5080猫头鹰版则完全采用了不同的设计哲学。这款显卡的核心目标就是极致低噪，为此两家公司不惜工本与体积，致力于打造目前可能最安静的GPU风冷散热器。

本次RTX 5080猫头鹰版首次在合作产品中采用了三颗猫头鹰NF-A12x25 G2风扇。该风扇集成了猫头鹰标志性的尖端工程优化，其设计细致入微：每片扇叶的曲率、风扇框体的导流槽以及扇叶尖端的涡流控制小翼，均经过精密计算，旨在优化气流分布与均匀性，减少湍流。其扇叶与框架的间隙极小，甚至难以塞入一张打印纸。为进一步优化噪音特性，猫头鹰为此显卡提供了两种略有转速差的NF-A12x25 G2风扇，以避免因频率同步而产生的周期性嗡鸣或共振。

这些风扇与一个庞大的定制散热器相连。该散热器采用均热板底座，并贯穿了11根热管，连接着一个长达14.5英寸（约36.8厘米）的密集鳍片堆。巨大的均热板全面覆盖了GB203 GPU核心以及环绕其周围的GDDR7显存。显卡的VRM供电模组和电感也通过独立的金属接触板及导热垫与鳍片堆相连，构成完整的热管理方案。

最终成果是一个庞然大物：显卡重达5.9磅（约2.7公斤），尺寸为38.2 x 13.6 x 8.1厘米，完全占据了四个PCIe插槽的高度（其中约两个槽位被风扇本身占用），在体积和重量上远超公版（Founders Edition）设计。

尽管追求静音，但RTX 5080猫头鹰版的设计美学颇具争议。其塑料风扇罩和金属背板均采用了猫头鹰标志性的哑光棕色，并带有在直射光下才显现的细微闪点。标志性的棕米配色风扇深受猫头鹰粉丝热爱，但也可能令不熟悉该品牌的用户感到不适应。细节之处体现了猫头鹰的品牌元素，如正面暗示翅膀或眉毛的金属装饰，以及背板通风口借用的猫头鹰logo中的猫头鹰半脸轮廓。背板上的条纹涂装工艺精湛，模拟出了压印或浮雕的视觉效果。

从规格对比来看，RTX 5080猫头鹰版与公版卡搭载相同的GPU核心（GB203），拥有相同的10752个CUDA核心和16GB GDDR7显存。但其加速频率略高（2700 MHz vs 2617 MHz），因此在峰值FP32算力上略有优势（58.1 TFLOPS vs 56.3 TFLOPS）。双方显卡的TGP均为360瓦，使用相同的12V-2x6电源接口。然而，猫头鹰版的建议零售价高达1699.99美元，远超公版卡的999.99美元，溢价部分显然用于支付其极度复杂的散热系统与品牌合作价值。

总而言之，华硕RTX 5080猫头鹰版是一款为极致静音需求而生的、不计体积与成本的顶级风冷显卡。它通过猫头鹰顶级的风扇工程技术、庞大的均热板与热管散热矩阵，重新定义了高端GPU风冷散热的性能与静音标准。它非常适合用于需要强大图形性能，同时对系统噪音极其敏感的工作站、家庭影院PC或发烧友静音装机。其独特的棕色外观是鲜明的品牌宣言，在猫头鹰主题构建中堪称完美，但也可能限制了它在追求色彩统一的主流装机中的普及。未来若能推出全黑化的Chromax版本，或许能吸引更广泛的用户群体。

items.title: Arc Raiders was accidentally recording Discord conversations into an unencrypted local game file — vulnerability in SDK could log messages and credentials in plaintext
items.description: A vulnerability in Discord's SDK allowed Arc Raiders to unintentionally log private DMs and user account credentials to the game's log files. Embark Studios has since hotfixed the problem.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A computer engineer has discovered a serious vulnerability within Discord's SDK that allows games to store Discord DMs between players in game logs without any security measures. System engineer Timothy Meadows published a blog post, revealing an incident where Arc Raiders was storing DMs between two gamers in plaintext to a local log file. Thankfully, at the time of writing, the problem has since been hotfixed by Embark Studios.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCTimothy discovered that Arc Raiders' Discord SDK was using a completely unencrypted bearer token and logs "all events" including any private conversations to the user's local drive without any encryption. A bearer token stores the user's Discord credentials, and anyone who gets this token has full access to the Discord user's account, including private DMs, friends list, and account settings.This is made worse by the fact that if Arc Raiders crashes and the user sends log files to Embark Studios (the game's development team), the company's employees will have that user's full account credentials and any DMs that were sent to the log files. You may like Steam client allegedly continues sharing your status with your friends even if you set it ‘Offline,’ report claims Security researcher says AMD auto-updater downloads software insecurely, enabling remote code execution User accidentally gains control of over 6,700 robot vacuums while tinkering with their own device Arc Raider uses the Discord SDK to show your Discord friends list in-game and invite Discord friends to the game. For this limited functionality, Timothy states the game only requires a "limited OAuth scope for game activity display." This would solve the issue and stop Arc Raiders from recording DMs to log files and storing a user's full account credentials to the game's log files as well. Some engineers who've inspected Discord's API say the issue lies solely with Discord, however.I dug into the ARC Raiders Discord token leak issue; this might not be ARC Raiders or Embark's fault. Discord's new Social SDK has a logging hook you can override, and as far as I can tell Discord is failing to scrub log events of sensitive information. API: discord.com/developers/d...— @eidolon.photon.institute (@eidolon.photon.institute.bsky.social) 2026-03-05T19:48:37.434ZThankfully, Embark Studios has since patched the issue with a hotfix. The game company assured users that no private or personal data was sent outside of gamers' PCs, and the company itself has not reviewed or kept any personal information that might have been sent to them. Embark Studios has completely disabled Discord's SDK and is conducting an audit to ensure that there are no other problems with the SDK.This isn't the first time Discord has to deal with security issues. The social app was hacked by a ransomware group late last year, demanding $3.5 million from Discord's developers, and allegedly stole 70,000 government ID photos. items.titleZh: 《Arc Raiders》意外将Discord对话记录至本地未加密游戏文件——SDK安全漏洞致聊天内容与凭证以明文存储
items.descriptionZh:
一名计算机工程师在Discord的软件开发工具包（SDK）中发现了一个严重的安全漏洞，该漏洞允许游戏在没有任何安全措施的情况下，将玩家之间的Discord私密聊天记录存储于游戏日志中。系统工程师蒂莫西·梅多斯发布了一篇博客文章，披露了游戏《Arc Raiders》将两名玩家之间的私信以明文形式记录到本地日志文件的事件。值得庆幸的是，截至发稿时，开发商Embark Studios已通过热修复解决了该问题。

蒂莫西发现，《Arc Raiders》使用的Discord SDK在记录“所有事件”时，使用了一个完全未加密的承载令牌，并将包括任何私人对话在内的信息以未加密形式写入用户本地驱动器。承载令牌存储了用户的Discord凭证，任何获得此令牌的人都将拥有对该Discord账户的完全访问权限，包括私信、好友列表和账户设置。

更严重的是，如果《Arc Raiders》游戏崩溃，用户将日志文件发送给Embark Studios（游戏开发团队），该公司的员工就将获得该用户的完整账户凭证以及记录在日志文件中的所有私信内容。

《Arc Raiders》使用Discord SDK是为了在游戏中显示Discord好友列表并邀请Discord好友加入游戏。蒂莫西指出，为实现这种有限的功能，游戏本只需申请“用于显示游戏活动的有限OAuth授权范围”。这样就能解决问题，阻止游戏将私信记录到日志文件，并避免将用户的完整账户凭证存储于游戏日志文件中。

不过，一些检查过Discord API的工程师表示，问题可能完全出在Discord本身。有工程师深入调查后指出，Discord新的社交SDK包含一个可被覆盖的日志钩子，而据其判断，Discord未能从日志事件中清除敏感信息。这意味着责任可能在于Discord API的设计，而非游戏开发商。

所幸，Embark Studios已通过热修复修补了该漏洞。该公司向用户保证，没有私人或个人数据被发送到玩家电脑之外，公司本身也未曾审查或保留任何可能发送给他们的个人信息。Embark Studios目前已完全禁用了Discord的SDK，并正在进行审计，以确保该SDK没有其他问题。

这并非Discord首次面临安全问题。去年年底，该社交应用曾遭勒索软件组织攻击，攻击者向Discord开发者索要350万美元，并据称窃取了7万张政府身份证照片。此次SDK漏洞事件再次引发了对其平台安全性的关注。对于将第三方社交功能集成到游戏中的开发者而言，这起事件突显了严格审查API权限和数据处理流程的重要性，同时也提醒用户需警惕游戏可能过度请求权限的行为。

items.title: US gov't preps sweeping export controls for Nvidia, AMD AI hardware — worldwide licensing system would give Trump admin broad authority to block global sales
items.description: The Trump administration is working on sweeping export regulations over AI hardware that will make large AI clusters a topic of intergovernmental talks.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The U.S. government is drafting sweeping new export regulations that would give it authority to approve nearly all global shipments of advanced AI accelerators made by American companies, including AMD and Nvidia, reports Bloomberg. The rules would expand existing country-based restrictions into a worldwide licensing system and will give Trump's administration power to allow or decline large-scale AI infrastructure buildouts. The rule turns out to be considerably stricter than the highly-criticized AI Diffusion Rule from the Biden era. Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe newly proposed export regime establishes a tiered licensing system based on computing scale: small-scale batches would pass through a simplified review, mid-scale deployments would require 'preclearance before seeking export licenses' from the U.S. Department of Commerce, whereas large-scale AI clusters would require governments of host countries to negotiate the deal with the U.S. government and investments in the U.S. AI infrastructure as part of the deal.Shipments involving up to 1,000 Nvidia GB300 GPUs would pass through a simplified review and may qualify for limited exemptions.Larger installations would require pre-authorization before export licenses could be issued. These will introduce compliance obligations that may include operational transparency, disclosure of business activities, and potential on-site inspections by U.S. authorities.Clusters powered by 200,000 GB300 GPUs operated by a single company within one country — such as those currently deployed by AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, Open AI, or xAI — would trigger direct intergovernmental arrangements. In these cases, approvals would depend on national-security assurances as well as commitments to invest in American AI infrastructure.For now, companies like AMD, Cerebras, and Nvidia cannot ship high-performance AI processors to select countries, such as China, Iran, North Korea, and Russia, just to name a few. Yet, shipping large-scale batches of AI accelerators to Middle Eastern countries requires direct intergovernmental arrangements and investments in the American AI infrastructure. If the newly proposed regulation becomes a law, then such arrangements would become much more common particularly with allied nations in Europe. You may like US Commerce Department confirms harsh new AI export rules, shoots down reports over the return of Biden-era AI Diffusion rule Analyzing Washington's new AI accelerator export rules U.S. posts official H200 and MI325X AI GPU export rules to China, but with plenty of caveats The proposed policy is by no means a total export ban, but an extremely powerful tool that lets the U.S. government influence how global AI infrastructure develops. If the Trump administration does not like the fact that a UK or France-based company deploys a cluster of over 200,000 GB300 GPUs or equivalent, it can deny appropriate export licenses if appropriate governments and companies do not meet their requirements.If the proposed policy is enacted, for companies like AMD or Nvidia, which ship their products globally, everything will depend on how quickly licenses are granted and what conditions are attached. Fast approvals with light restrictions would let most small projects move forward, though with more paperwork. However, for larger installations, tougher requirements would delay construction and make operating large data centers outside the U.S. considerably more complicated. Meanwhile, building clusters comparable to those currently operated by AWS, Google, Oracle, Microsoft, or xAI outside of the U.S. would face extreme complications, which would lower their economic feasibility. items.titleZh: 特朗普政府准备启动对英伟达、AMD人工智能硬件的全面出口管制——全球许可制度将赋予其阻止全球销售的广泛授权
items.descriptionZh:
根据彭博社报道，美国政府正在起草全面的新出口管制法规，该法规将赋予其审批几乎所有由美国公司（包括AMD和英伟达）生产的先进AI加速器全球出货的权力。新规将把现有的基于国别的限制扩展为全球性的许可体系，并使特朗普政府有权批准或拒绝大规模AI基础设施的建设。这项规定被证明比备受批评的拜登时代的《AI扩散规则》要严格得多。

新提出的出口制度建立了一个基于计算规模的分级许可体系：小规模批次的货物将通过简化审查；中等规模部署需要在美国商务部“寻求出口许可证前获得预批准”；而大型AI集群则需要东道国政府与美国政府进行谈判，并将投资美国AI基础设施作为交易的一部分。

具体而言，涉及多达1000个英伟达GB300 GPU的发货将通过简化审查，并可能有资格获得有限豁免。更大型的安装则需要获得预授权才能颁发出口许可证。这将引入合规义务，可能包括运营透明度、商业活动披露以及美国当局的潜在现场检查。

由单个国家内一家公司运营的、配备20万个GB300 GPU的集群（例如目前由AWS、微软、甲骨文、OpenAI或xAI部署的集群）将触发直接的政府间安排。在此类情况下，批准将取决于国家安全保证以及对投资美国AI基础设施的承诺。

目前，AMD、Cerebras和英伟达等公司已不能向中国、伊朗、朝鲜和俄罗斯等特定国家运送高性能AI处理器。然而，向中东国家运送大批量AI加速器已经需要直接的政府间安排和对美国AI基础设施的投资。如果新提议的法规成为法律，那么此类安排将变得更为普遍，尤其是与欧洲的盟国之间。

拟议的政策绝非全面出口禁令，而是一个极其强大的工具，使美国政府能够影响全球AI基础设施的发展方式。如果特朗普政府不满意英国或法国公司部署超过20万个GB300 GPU（或同等算力）的集群，且相关政府和公司未满足其要求，它可以拒绝发放相应的出口许可证。

如果拟议政策颁布，对于像AMD或英伟达这样在全球销售产品的公司来说，一切将取决于许可证的批准速度以及附加的条件。快速批准和宽松限制将使大多数小型项目得以推进，尽管需要更多文书工作。然而，对于大型安装项目，更严格的要求将延迟建设，并使在美国境外运营大型数据中心变得复杂得多。同时，在美国境外建设与目前由AWS、谷歌、甲骨文、微软或xAI运营的规模相当的集群将面临极大困难，从而降低其经济可行性。

items.title: As Frank Yeary retires from Intel, the company picks an engineer to chair its board — Intel Foundry governance issues to be resolved, and looking back at the Yeary years
items.description: Intel’s board of directors is getting a new independent chair, effective May 13, following the company’s Annual Stockholders’ Meeting.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Intel’s board of directors is getting a new independent chair effective May 13, after the company’s Annual Stockholders’ Meeting, the company announced earlier this week. Dr. Craig H. Barratt, a semiconductor engineer by training — not to be confused with former CEO Craig R. Barrett — will assume the role, succeeding finance veteran Frank D. Yeary, who has served on the board since 2009, and as chair since 2023.Intel made the announcement on March 3, clarifying that Yeary will not stand for reelection in May. It's the most consequential governance change at Intel since the board’s forced exit of former CEO Pat Gelsinger in late 2024. It also consolidates authority around Barratt, who will lead the board’s focus on scaling U.S. R&D and manufacturing, and CEO Lip-Bu Tan, who has publicly supported Intel Foundry since taking the role.Who is Craig H. Barratt?Barratt, 63, holds bachelor's degrees in pure mathematics, physics, and electrical engineering from the University of Sydney, as well as a master's and doctorate in electrical engineering from Stanford. You may like Intel Chairman Frank Yeary retires, Craig Barrat to become the new chairman of the Board of Directors Intel Foundry boss leaves for Qualcomm Intel appoints Trump economic advisor as head of government affairs He joined Atheros Communications in 2002 as vice president of technology, was appointed president and CEO in 2003, and led the company for eight years. Atheros was the dominant supplier of Wi-Fi chipsets during the early 802.11 boom, shipping silicon into PCs, consumer routers, smartphones, and networking gear. Under Barratt, the company went public in 2004 and was acquired by Qualcomm in 2011 for roughly $3.1 billion. He then served as president of Qualcomm Atheros through 2013, overseeing the combined non-cellular connectivity and networking silicon business.After leaving Qualcomm, Barratt ran Google Access and Energy as senior vice president from 2012 to 2016, overseeing Google Fiber and related internet infrastructure projects. He then took over as CEO of Barefoot Networks, a startup building programmable Ethernet switching ASICs, from 2017 until Intel acquired the company in 2019. Post-acquisition, he became senior vice president and general manager of Intel's Ethernet, silicon photonics, and networking businesses, a role he held through 2020. He joined Intel’s board as an independent director in November 2025.None of that is related to CPU or process technology, though; Barratt has never run a foundry or managed a node transition. What he has done is build and sell semiconductor businesses, work inside Intel as an executive, and, most recently, sit on the board of a company whose products are deeply embedded in the AI infrastructure supply chain — something Intel is trying to break into with its Gaudi accelerators and Intel Foundry.One of Intel’s ‘most consequential’ periods (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Yeary, by contrast, came from investment banking and financial advisory, having spent more than two decades in those fields before joining Intel's board in 2009. His tenure as chair, beginning in 2023, coincided with the most financially damaging stretch in the company's recent history, with losses exceeding $16.6 billion in Q3 2024 alone, a market capitalization that fell dramatically, and a floundering foundry operation that has not yet approached profitability.Yeary took consequential actions during that period and is understood to have orchestrated Gelsinger's removal. He then brought in Lip-Bu Tan and shepherded an intentional board refresh that has added four independent directors with technology and operational backgrounds since 2024.“Frank led the effort to bring me in as the company’s CEO, encouraged disciplined board oversight, and reinforced strong board governance. With his and the board’s support, I have been empowered to take decisive actions to strengthen our financial foundation, advance our process roadmap, and position the company for long-term competitiveness,” said Tan in an official press release announcing Yeary’s retirement.But some industry analysts have reported that Yeary and Tan were not aligned on keeping Intel Foundry. In a post to X on August 7, Citrini Research analyst Jukan cited insiders who claimed that Yeary drafted a plan to spin off Intel Foundry as an independent entity, bring in minority investment from companies including Nvidia and Amazon, and effectively step back from contract manufacturing as a core business. What to read next Eric Demers leaves for Intel after 14 years at Qualcomm Intel CEO recognizes its 18A node for external customers as 18A-P gets 'inbound interest' President Trump, Sec. Lutnick praise Intel after the launch of Panther Lake chips "With a stronger balance sheet, meaningful progress across our roadmap — including Intel 18A and 14A — and a clear path forward under Lip-Bu, this is the appropriate time for me to step down as chair and from the board and transition leadership to a new independent chair,” said Yeary.Almost at the start of his term, CEO Tan and Board Chairman Frank Yeary were split over whether to continue Intel’s semiconductor manufacturing and foundry business or to exit it.Chairman Yeary had drafted a plan to spin off the loss-making foundry business and invite minority… pic.twitter.com/rRQjKJzSyFAugust 8, 2025Tan opposed it, arguing that the foundry is both “essential to Intel’s success and necessary for the U.S. to secure” a domestic alternative to TSMC. None of this has been acknowledged by Intel, however, so it should be read with appropriate caution.What’s confirmed is that Yeary is leaving, and the new chair has publicly stated that his focus will be on supporting “rigorous execution” in investing and scaling U.S. R&D and manufacturing.Proving 18A at scale The 288-core Intel Clearwater Forest Xeon CPU, based on the company's 18A process. (Image credit: Intel)Tan has staked Intel’s recovery on first proving that 18A can work at scale, and second, convincing external customers to commit to Intel Foundry for production. Neither objective is achievable if the board retains any serious appetite for spinning out or selling the Intel Foundry business, which posted an operating loss of $13.4 billion in 2024.Intel launched Panther Lake (Core Ultra Series 3) on 18A at CES in January, with consumer systems shipping later that month, making it the first commercial platform built on the node and the most advanced process ever manufactured in the United States, using RibbonFET gate-all-around transistors and PowerVia backside power delivery.Yields are sufficient to support Panther Lake shipments, but Intel CFO David Zinsner said in October that they are not yet high enough to deliver normal profit margins, with industry-standard yield results not expected until 2027. It’s understood that Intel doesn’t plan to add significant 18A capacity in 2026 beyond current commitments.In Barratt, Intel will gain a chair who has publicly committed to scaling U.S. manufacturing, who operates inside a semiconductor ecosystem that depends on foundry diversity, and who has worked inside Intel's own infrastructure business. That’ll obviously matter to potential foundry customers doing long-term planning who need confidence that Intel Foundry will still exist and be resourced in five years."The company has taken significant steps to strengthen its financial position, advance its technology and product roadmap, and enhance operational discipline," said Barratt. "The board thanks Frank for his leadership and for helping position Intel for this next phase."Intel's board will also shrink from 12 to 11 members following the May meeting, as Yeary's seat will not be filled. Given that four new directors with technology operating backgrounds have joined the board since 2024, its overall composition is moving in a consistent direction away from purely financial oversight and towards technical expertise.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 弗兰克·耶里从英特尔退休，公司任命工程师担任董事长——英特尔代工治理问题待解，回顾耶里任期
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔公司于本周宣布，其董事会将迎来一位新的独立主席。自5月13日年度股东大会结束后，拥有半导体工程师背景的克雷格·H·巴雷特博士将正式接任此职，接替自2009年加入董事会、并于2023年起担任主席的金融界资深人士弗兰克·D·耶尔。这是自2024年底董事会迫使前CEO帕特·基辛格离职以来，英特尔最具影响力的治理层变动，旨在将权力巩固于新任主席巴雷特和公开支持英特尔代工业务的CEO Lip-Bu Tan周围，以共同推动美国研发与制造规模的扩大。

**背景与治理层变动的深层原因**
此次变动发生在英特尔的关键转折期。公司正经历近年来最具财务挑战的阶段，仅2024年第三季度就录得超过166亿美元的亏损，市值大幅缩水，而其寄予厚望的代工业务至今仍未接近盈利。在此背景下，董事会主席耶尔被认为主导了前CEO基辛格的去职，并引进了现任CEO Tan。尽管Tan在官方声明中感谢耶尔的支持，但有行业分析师透露，二人曾在是否保留英特尔代工业务这一核心战略上存在根本分歧。据称，耶尔曾起草计划，意图将亏损的代工业务剥离为独立实体，并引入包括英伟达和亚马逊在内的少数股权投资，从而实质上退出合同制造这一核心业务。而CEO Tan则坚决反对，认为代工业务对英特尔自身的成功以及美国建立台积电之外的国内先进制程替代选项都至关重要。虽然英特尔官方未承认此内部分歧，但耶尔的离任与巴雷特的上任，无疑标志着董事会战略方向的统一。

**新任主席的技术与商业背景**
新任主席克雷格·H·巴雷特现年63岁，拥有悉尼大学的纯数学、物理和电气工程学士学位，以及斯坦福大学的电气工程硕士和博士学位。他的职业生涯与CPU或制程技术的直接管理关联不大，但深植于半导体与网络技术生态。其关键履历包括：
1.  **Atheros Communications：** 2003年至2011年担任总裁兼CEO，领导这家公司在早期802.11 Wi-Fi热潮中成为主导的芯片组供应商，并于2011年以约31亿美元的价格被高通收购。
2.  **高通与谷歌：** 收购后担任高通Atheros总裁至2013年；2012年至2016年担任谷歌高级副总裁，负责谷歌光纤（Google Fiber）及相关互联网基础设施项目。
3.  **Barefoot Networks与英特尔：** 2017年至2019年担任可编程以太网交换芯片初创公司Barefoot Networks的CEO，直至其被英特尔收购。随后在英特尔内部担任高级副总裁兼总经理，主管以太网、硅光子和网络业务至2020年。他于2025年11月以独立董事身份加入英特尔董事会。

巴雷特的背景显示出，他擅长构建和出售半导体业务，拥有在英特尔内部担任高管的经验，并且其职业生涯与深度嵌入AI基础设施供应链的网络和连接技术密切相关——这正是英特尔试图通过其Gaudi加速器和代工业务切入的领域。

**技术路线与当前挑战：押注18A制程**
CEO Tan将英特尔的复苏押注于两个核心目标：首先是证明其18A制程能够实现大规模、可靠的生产；其次是说服外部客户承诺使用英特尔代工进行量产。2025年1月，英特尔在CES上发布了基于18A制程的Panther Lake（酷睿Ultra第3代）客户端平台，这不仅是该节点的首款商用产品，也是美国本土制造过的、采用RibbonFET全环绕栅极晶体管和PowerVia背面供电技术的最先进制程芯片。
然而，挑战依然严峻。尽管18A的良率已足以支持Panther Lake的出货，但英特尔CFO大卫·津斯纳在去年10月表示，该良率尚不足以带来正常的利润率，行业标准水平的良率预计要到2027年才能实现。此外，英特尔在2024年录得了高达134亿美元的代工业务运营亏损。公司理解，在实现可持续盈利之前，大规模扩张18A产能并不现实。

**影响与未来展望**
巴雷特的上任向市场和潜在客户传递了几个明确信号：
1.  **战略延续性：** 巴雷特已公开承诺将支持“严格投资和扩大美国研发与制造规模的执行力”。这直接回应了关于代工业务可能被剥离的猜测，旨在给予那些需要进行长期规划的潜在代工客户以信心，确保英特尔代工业务在未来五年内仍将存在并获得资源支持。
2.  **董事会构成转变：** 随着耶尔离任且其席位不再填补，董事会成员将从12人缩减至11人。更重要的是，自2024年以来，已有四位拥有技术运营背景的新独立董事加入。这表明英特尔董事会的整体构成正从纯粹的财务监督，持续向具备深厚技术专业知识的方向转变。
3.  **生态连接优势：** 巴雷特在高度依赖代工多样性的半导体生态系统中的丰富经验，以及他曾管理英特尔内部基础设施业务的经历，被认为有助于其理解客户需求并推动代工业务的发展。

此次领导层更迭，标志着英特尔在经历财务和战略动荡后，试图通过强化技术领导、统一董事会意志并坚定不移地推进其最先进的18A/14A制程和代工战略，来重回竞争轨道。能否在严苛的技术执行与财务平衡中取得成功，将是巴雷特与Tan领导团队面临的核心考验。

items.title: China's top chip execs claim ASML alternative 'small, fragmented, and weak' — Chinese industry titans call for national effort to invest in advanced chipmaking tools
items.description: China's most senior semiconductor executives issued a public call this week for a consolidated national effort to build a domestic alternative to Dutch lithography giant ASML.

Subscribe to our newsletter China's most senior semiconductor executives issued a public call this week for a consolidated national effort to build a domestic alternative to Dutch lithography giant ASML, warning that the country's chip equipment industry remains too "small, fragmented, and weak" to overcome U.S. export restrictions on its own.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingIn remarks co-authored by SMIC co-founder Wang Yangyuan alongside leaders of memory giant YMTC, chip equipment maker Naura, and EDA software developer Empyrean, three specific areas where U.S. export controls have choked China's semiconductor ambitions were identified: electronic design automation software, silicon wafers, and manufacturing equipment — particularly extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography, which enables the sub-7nm chip production that China currently cannot replicate.As reported by the South China Morning Post, the authors urged the industry to "abandon illusions and prepare for struggle," and argued that fragmented public funding was dispersing resources across too many competing efforts without producing results. You may like Chinese fabs are reportedly upgrading older ASML DUV lithography chipmaking machines U.S. lawmakers demand sales ban on chipmaking tools to China China's reverse-engineered Frankenstein EUV chipmaking tool hasn't produced a single chip China is currently drafting its 15th Five-Year Plan, which is set to be revealed to the National People’s Congress within the next week and covers 2026 to 2030. It’s widely expected that this iteration of the plan will prioritize lithography breakthroughs and EDA tool development as national targets. Big Fund III, a state-backed vehicle with roughly $47.5 billion earmarked for semiconductors, has already redirected fresh capital toward lithography and EDA as substitutes for ASML and Synopsys tools, respectively.The sheer candor about fragmentation from this collection of executives is difficult to ignore, however, given just how often we see big, bold tech claims coming from Chinese media, but it aligns with an increasingly obvious challenge. China's most advanced domestically produced DUV lithography system, from Yuliangsheng, is technically comparable to ASML's Twinscan NXT:1950i — a machine ASML originally designed for 32nm-class processes back in 2008.Even if SMIC manages to integrate that tool into a 28nm process by 2027, reaching sub-10nm would require redesigned scanners and several additional years of development. A prototype EUV machine has reportedly been completed in a Shenzhen lab, but EUV's commercial viability requires solving yield challenges that took ASML nearly two decades to overcome after its own prototype.That’s all before we’ve even considered ASML’s accumulated know-how as, in Yangyuan’s words, “merely the integrator.” The company’s EUV dominance rests on a supply chain of more than 5,000 subcontractors, along with decades of high-volume manufacturing data. No amount of reverse engineering can quickly replicate that. While it’s true that Chinese firms have made real gains in adjacent equipment categories — Naura, for example, is one of the world’s top ten semi equipment vendors by revenue — lithography remains well out of reach.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 中国芯片高管称ASML替代品"小、散、弱"——行业巨头呼吁举全国之力投资先进芯片制造设备
items.descriptionZh:
本周，中国半导体行业多位顶尖高管联合发表公开呼吁，要求国家集中力量打造国产化光刻机替代方案，以抗衡荷兰光刻巨头ASML。中芯国际联合创始人王阳元、存储巨头长江存储、设备商北方华创及EDA软件公司华大九天等企业负责人共同指出，当前中国芯片设备产业仍存在“规模小、布局散、实力弱”的突出问题，难以单独突破美国出口管制带来的封锁。

文章深入剖析了美国出口管制在三个关键领域对中国半导体发展的制约：电子设计自动化（EDA）软件、硅晶圆及制造设备——尤其是极紫外（EUV）光刻技术。该技术是实现7纳米以下芯片量产的关键，而中国目前尚未掌握自主生产能力。

高管们在《南华早报》的报道中警示行业需“抛弃幻想、准备斗争”，并指出当前分散的公共资金投入导致资源过度摊薄，众多竞争性研发项目难以形成有效突破。

当前中国正在起草第十五个五年规划（2026-2030年），预计将于近期提交全国人大审议。业界普遍预期该规划将把光刻技术突破和EDA工具研发列为国家优先目标。规模约475亿美元的国家集成电路产业投资基金三期已明确将资金导向光刻机和EDA领域，旨在分别替代ASML和新思科技的产品。

尽管中国媒体时常发布重大技术突破的声明，但此次行业领袖对产业分散现状的坦承尤为值得关注。目前中国最先进的国产深紫外（DUV）光刻系统（源于玉梁盛科技）技术指标仅相当于ASML 2008年推出的Twinscan NXT:1950i设备，该设备原设计用于32纳米级工艺。

即使中芯国际能在2027年前将该设备整合至28纳米工艺，要实现10纳米以下制程仍需重新设计扫描系统并投入数年研发时间。尽管有报道称深圳实验室已完成EUV原型机，但要实现商业应用必须解决良率问题——ASML当年从原型机到成熟量产足足耗费了近二十年。

更关键的是，正如王阳元所言，ASML本质上是“系统集成者”。其EUV技术主导地位建立在超过5000家供应商构成的供应链体系，以及数十年来积累的海量量产数据基础之上，这种系统化能力绝非通过反向工程可以快速复制。虽然中国企业在相邻设备领域已取得实质性进展（如北方华创已跻身全球半导体设备营收前十），但在光刻机领域仍存在显著差距。

与此同时，中国晶圆厂正尝试对现有ASML DUV设备进行升级改造，而美国议员则持续施压要求扩大对华芯片制造设备禁售范围。行业现实表明，中国在光刻技术领域的自主化道路仍面临严峻挑战。

items.title: ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI, shortages — Lisa Su says business exceeded expectations while Intel is looking at long-term agreements with potential customers
items.description: Both AMD and Intel reports increased demand for CPUs, driven by AI agents' need for general computing power.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 21 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Both AMD and Intel said during the 2026 Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference that demand for CPUs is seeing an uptick due to artificial intelligence. Intel CFO David Zinsner said during his question and answer (via Investing.com) that “the CPU has become cool again this year,” especially as AI agents need CPUs to orchestrate the computationally-heavy tasks that the GPUs and NPUs will execute. It has even started seeing customers who are looking at long-term agreements, ensuring that they’ll have a continuous supply of these chips needed to expand their operations. On the other hand, AMD CEO Lisa Su said during the same conference, “You know, we’re seeing a significant CPU demand, frankly, as a result of the inference demand picking up.” She also added later that “the CPU portion of the business has actually far exceeded my expectations in terms of demand.”Go deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptThe AI boom has been driving a shortage in various components since ChatGPT showed some potential in late 2022. It first began with GPUs, especially as data centers and hyperscalers have been buying these components en masse to build massive servers with hundreds of thousands of GPUs. As graphics card supply started to normalize around the middle of 2025, experts and analysts have started warning about memory and storage chip shortages due to massive demand for high-bandwidth memory and enterprise-grade storage in AI data centers.We felt the full swing of this crisis in the fourth quarter of last year, with pricing for RAM modules and SSDs continuing to rise through February 2026. What makes this worse than the GPU shortage is that it has a much wider impact. While the GPUs that were in short supply were mostly limited to desktop PCs and gaming laptops with a discrete graphics card, virtually every modern digital device — from consumer devices like smart TVs and smartphones to automobiles and industrial-grade equipment — needs memory and storage. And consumer-grade memory and storage is fighting for the same wafer space that enterprise-grade memory and storage could occupy, usually with a far higher price tag. You may like Intel, AMD server CPUs reportedly suffering from supply shortages in China AMD Zen 6 and Intel Nova Lake CPUs reportedly arriving late, delayed to CES 2027 'We can't completely vacate the client market' says Intel amid wafer supply shortages As AI advancements move forward from large language models and chatbots to agents that can observe, reason, plan, act, and learn independently, data centers require more multi-processor computing power — that means combining CPUs, GPUs, NPUs, and more — to support the entire agentic AI workflow. China is starting to see this spike in demand, with both Team Blue and Team Red reporting supply shortages for server CPUs in the region. We’re also seeing a spike in demand for high-end Mac Studios and Mac minis, especially as we see more people build their own local AI agents with the rise in popularity of the open-source Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw.AMD and Intel are presumably talking about data center demand for their CPUs; consumer systems aren't equipped to handle the massive memory demands of agentic AI. If there is a shortage, however, it could trickle down to the consumer market, assuming the demand keeps pace.Over the past several generations, AMD and Intel have converged their data center and consumer offerings, allowing them to maximize yields by leveraging the same microarchitecture across both client and enterprise. Some of that silicon won't be useful in the data center, so the consumer market won't evaporate. But it could put downward pressure on supply if the focus shifts toward the data center, as we've seen with RAM and SSDs.Unlike Nvidia, which has seen exponential increases in revenue in its data center business, both AMD and Intel still see about half of their total revenue each quarter from the consumer market. It's still an important market, so although demand from data centers may increase, it shouldn't come at the cost of the consumer market, at least entirely.Hopefully, both Intel and AMD can keep up with the future demand, so as not to exacerbate the worsening situation of the computer industry. Otherwise, some are already predicting the end of the entry-level PC by 2028 if things continue as they are.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: CPU需求因AI智能体激增而再度火热，供不应求——苏姿丰称业务超预期，英特尔寻求与潜在客户签订长期协议
items.descriptionZh:
在2026年摩根士丹利技术、媒体与电信大会上，AMD和英特尔均指出，由于人工智能的蓬勃发展，市场对CPU的需求正在显著回升。英特尔首席财务官大卫·津斯纳表示，“CPU今年再次变得炙手可热”，特别是因为AI智能体需要CPU来协调和调度由GPU及NPU执行的计算密集型任务。他甚至提到，公司已开始接触寻求长期供货协议的客户，以确保其业务扩展能获得持续的芯片供应。AMD首席执行官苏姿丰在同一场合坦言，“我们看到CPU需求显著增长，这实际上是由于AI推理需求上升所驱动的。”她随后补充道，“CPU业务部分的实际需求远超我最初的预期。”

这一趋势的背景是，自2022年底ChatGPT展现出潜力以来，AI热潮已引发多轮关键组件短缺。最初是GPU，尤其是数据中心和超大规模用户批量采购以构建搭载数十万GPU的大型服务器。到2025年中，显卡供应开始趋于正常化，但专家和分析师转而警告，由于AI数据中心对高带宽内存和企业级存储的巨量需求，内存和存储芯片可能出现短缺。

这场危机的全面影响在去年第四季度显现，RAM模块和SSD的价格持续上涨至2026年2月。与GPU短缺相比，此次短缺的影响范围更广。GPU短缺主要局限于配备独立显卡的台式机和游戏笔记本，而内存和存储芯片则几乎是所有现代数字设备——从智能电视、智能手机到汽车和工业设备——都必需的组件。消费级内存和存储产品正在与价格通常高得多的企业级产品争夺相同的晶圆产能。

随着AI技术从大型语言模型和聊天机器人，向能够观察、推理、规划、行动和自主学习的智能体演进，数据中心需要更强大的多处理器计算能力——即结合CPU、GPU、NPU等多种处理单元——以支持完整的智能体AI工作流程。中国市场已开始出现需求激增，英特尔和AMD均报告其服务器CPU在该地区供应短缺。同时，对高端Mac Studio和Mac mini的需求也出现飙升，这得益于开源AI项目（如Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw）的流行，促使更多人构建本地AI智能体。

AMD和英特尔所谈论的CPU需求增长主要针对数据中心领域；消费级系统通常不具备处理智能体AI所需的海量内存能力。然而，如果需求持续高涨，短缺效应可能会传导至消费市场。过去几代产品中，AMD和英特尔已将其数据中心和消费级产品的微架构进行融合，通过跨客户端和企业端使用相同设计来最大化晶圆产出。部分芯片可能不适合数据中心使用，因此消费市场不会完全消失。但如果产业重心向数据中心倾斜，消费级产品的供应可能会受到挤压，正如我们在RAM和SSD市场所见。

与英伟达数据中心业务收入呈指数级增长不同，AMD和英特尔每季度仍有约一半的总收入来自消费市场。这仍然是一个重要市场，因此尽管数据中心需求可能增长，但理论上不应以完全牺牲消费市场为代价。

业界希望英特尔和AMD能够跟上未来的需求，以避免加剧计算机行业本已严峻的形势。否则，若情况持续，已有人预测入门级PC市场可能在2028年走向终结。

items.title: Go beyond the review with Bench, the deepest consumer hardware benchmarking database on the internet — compare hundreds of products across a range of categories
items.description: Take advantage of Bench, the ultimate resource for PC hardware enthusiasts, offering a large database of benchmarks for you to browse through. We detail how the tool works, and what we're doing to keep results up-to-date here.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter In 2025, we introduced Tom's Hardware Premium, an even deeper dive into the world of tech and DIY computing, which you can access through our website for a small fee. While that includes exclusive news analysis, features and hardware roadmaps, the Premium offering also sports a large database of benchmark test results, named Bench.Bench is a performance analysis tool that gives you deeper access into how a given product performs in real-world and synthetic benchmarks. Using it, you can compare two different products in a given category, offering you the ability to view benchmark data between products with a simple click.Bench is a Tom's Hardware Premium exclusive feature, and you can get access to it — as well as everything THP has to offer — by subscribing now for as little as $3 a month. You may like Get Tom's Hardware Premium for less than $3 a month — access our hardware roadmaps, deeper analysis, exclusive features, and unlock the Bench database Behind the scenes of our massive CPU retest for Bench — testing at 1080p, choosing new apps, and gathering data for a decade of CPUs Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Tom's Hardware Premium Subcription: $29 at tomshardware.comDon’t miss out on Tom’s Hardware Premium. Get a full year of access for just $29: daily news analysis, deep dives into specialist topics in the semiconductor industry, as well as access to Bench, the largest benchmarking database around.View DealWith that out of the way, here's a sneak preview at what you can expect when subscribed. Let's take a look at a CPU comparison table to kick things off. Let's pit the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D against Intel's Core Ultra 9 285K.First, all I have to do is hit the compare tab and select the category, which spans CPUs, GPUs, Laptops, Monitors, and SSDs. Once we're in the CPU category, we can select the two products. Then you'll see a comparison between all of the data points we recorded for those two CPUs in alphabetical order.Image 1 of 3First, we select the category.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once the products are selected, you can scroll through benchmarks.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)There is a wealth of data to browse through.(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As well as the comparison tool, Bench offers tables of data per category. So, if you're looking to see which part performs the best in a certain workload, you can do that too.For the next example, we're going to pick out a specific GPU workload and see which consumer graphics card performs best in that scenario. Let's take the Sony hit God of War: Ragnarok. If you are building a mid-range system, then 1440p or QHD is a good spot to select for this benchmark. Since we're focused on the mid-range, let's take a look at the middle of the table. Here, we can see that the Radeon 9060 XT is faster than Nvidia's RTX 5060. These two GPUs hit around the same price point, so if I were a God of War fanatic looking to save, that would be my pick.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Toms Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)How we run BenchBench offers tons of data to browse through; these are just two examples of how you can use the tool to browse through hardware benchmarks. While the database is primarily built from the work completed while reviewing a given product, the data that you see in reviews is often truncated into geomean values, or put into a certain workload "bucket."Of course, while Bench has been live since last year, benchmarks don't stay up to date: Drivers change. New standards emerge. Therefore, we're undergoing a large-scale overhaul of our GPU and CPU tests, documenting exactly how we are benchmarking and what methodologies are being used.Recording benchmark values and taking the time to run the tests in the first place is inherently time-consuming, and Bench is the perfect reason to maintain a source of truth for all enthusiasts. What to read next Here are the tools I use as Tom’s Hardware resident CPU reviewer — a cheap aluminum open bench, thermal paste wipes, platform-labeled external power buttons, and more Hands-on with the Thermal Grizzly Der8enchtable: Updating our longstanding motherboard test bench The GPU benchmarks hierarchy 2026 As for our testing methodologies, here's a glimpse of what's next for our massive database.Revamped data for GPU testing (Image credit: Future)On the GPU side of things, Senior GPU Analyst Jeffrey Kampman has the reins. He's worked out exacting testing methodologies and strategies and outlined them in a separate Tom's Hardware Premium article. For those who are not yet subscribed, we've explained why we're not using frame generation or upscaling technologies like DLSS and FSR, and how we conduct real-world benchmarks. A canned benchmark is handy, but it doesn't represent how it really feels to play a game on the hardware. And more important, it's a more accurate measure of what to expect when playing those titles for yourself.As Jeff explains, he's spent years testing GPUs in both media and dedicated game testing labs, ensuring the integrity of the results on offer. The spread of titles being tested for 2026 is also representative of what players are actually playing, not merely popular benchmarking titles. For example, we've added the single-player epic Expedition 33, in addition to the multiplayer hit Arc Raiders. Adding newer titles to the list, across a range of engines and with Ray Tracing, allows us to build a cohesive outlook on how a given GPU performs in a wide range of scenarios. (Image credit: Nvidia)Updates will be going in phases, first from current-generation hardware like Nvidia's RTX 5000 series and AMD's 9000 series, all the way back to RTX 3000 and the Radeon 6000 lineup. Of course, Intel's offerings are in the mix there too — no matter how small the market share of the dedicated GPU, it'll be being tested for 2026.The three waves of updated tests will take around a month to fully complete, but will offer you a holistic look at the GPU market, and more importantly, offer you granular testing information that you simply can't get anywhere else.A herculean CPU retesting effort (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU Analyst Jake Roach is working alongside Editor-in-Chief and former CPU reviewer Paul Alcorn to deliver a gargantuan update to our CPU tests. Their goal is building a complete dataset of CPU releases that spans over a decade of hardware. For Premium subscribers, you can read Jake's entire methodology behind CPU testing here.Between two and six machines are running concurrently, with two Golden OS images, frozen and duplicated across setups, using the same testing hardware, down to the TIM, to ensure consistent results. Of course, as we are currently testing different platforms, the motherboards used between CPUs differ, but are consistent between rigs.While we are testing hundreds of CPUs, everything from the version of the software to the processes running in the background are identical. For the CPU effort, we'll be going from AMD Excavator and 7th-gen Intel to Zen 5 and Intel Arrow Lake. That's a lot of CPUs, and even more benchmarks. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Each modern CPU takes between 10 and 14 hours to test, with some older and slower models taking nearly an entire day, and with the number of chips on offer, it's going to take some time to get things up-to-date. As for the tests we run, things are split into several categories, such as applications, power/efficiency, and gaming workloads. While some tests are automated, each result is manually verified before being uploaded into our database and tools.Application testing goes across a wide range of workloads, from rendering and encoding to code compilation and data science. We're testing power efficiency across benchmarks like Y-Cruncher, Handbrake, and Prime95, just to name a few. Gaming testing is hands-on where possible. The selection of titles is broad and represents real-world players and what people are playing.As a result, no, you're not going to see something like Ashes of the Singularity, which no one plays, but is merely used as a benchmark. Instead, gaming benchmarks are selected across a range of popular titles like Monster Hunter: Wilds, DOOM: The Dark Ages, and Oblivion: Remastered, all of which use wholly different engines, and are being played by many today.Results will begin to trickle out across the database over time, and given the scale of the tests on offer, we will continue to evolve the benchmarking suite, while acknowledging that we might not revisit some of the older architectures on this list again.The benchmarks never stop (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On the backdrop of a challenging year for consumer hardware, no thanks to the ongoing AI buildout and supply challenges. Getting the most out of what's available is imperative, and Bench gives you access to all of the data you need for whatever workload or game you're looking at within a handful of clicks.We've put a lot of effort into building and maintaining this feature, and we will continue to do so, documenting the process and keeping things nice and fresh for Tom's Hardware Premium subscribers.Maintaining Bench without Tom's Hardware Premium simply isn't possible. It offers us the bandwidth to test, retest, and refine our methodologies outside of a standard CPU cycle or hierarchy update. It also becomes an invaluable reference point for those of you who wish to seek out specific hardware workloads, going beyond our standard reviews and wading through the data yourself.We're never going to stop testing, and Bench is the perfect place to compile our data. As we look forward to the next generation of PC hardware, we'll be sure to keep things up-to-date, giving you access to the cold, hard data behind our testing. In addition to our testing updates, we will also keep working behind the scenes to add new functionality to Bench in the coming months.Tom's Hardware Premium is priced at $3 a month when taking out an annual plan, or $7 for a rolling monthly subscription. Bench is just a single piece of what's on offer. To take advantage of the ultimate resource for enthusiasts, be sure to check it out today.

items.titleZh: 超越评测，体验Bench——互联网上最深入的消费级硬件评测数据库，比较覆盖各大品类的数百款产品。
items.descriptionZh:
知名科技媒体Tom's Hardware近期推出了一项名为“Bench”的硬件性能数据库服务，作为其付费订阅项目“Tom's Hardware Premium”的核心功能。这项服务旨在为硬件发烧友、DIY玩家和专业人士提供一个深度、全面且持续更新的硬件性能数据平台。

**背景与定位**
Tom's Hardware Premium是Tom's Hardware在2025年推出的深度订阅服务，用户支付少量费用（年付约合每月3美元）即可获取独家新闻分析、深度专题、硬件路线图以及庞大的基准测试数据库——Bench。在当前人工智能基建热潮导致消费级硬件市场面临挑战与供应链压力的背景下，帮助用户精准把握硬件性能、最大化利用现有资源变得尤为重要。Bench正是为此而生的工具，它汇聚了该媒体多年评测积累的海量数据，并致力于通过系统性的重测与更新，维持数据的时效性与准确性。

**核心功能与运作原理**
Bench本质上是一个性能分析工具，其核心创新在于将评测数据系统化、数据库化，并提供直观的交互比较。主要功能包括：
1.  **产品对比**：用户可轻松选择任意两个同类别产品（如CPU、GPU、笔记本等）进行横向对比。系统会罗列出所有相关的基准测试数据点，使用户能一目了然地查看各项性能差异。文中以AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D与Intel Core Ultra 9 285K的CPU对比为例进行了演示。
2.  **数据榜单**：除了对比，用户还可以按特定工作负载（如某款游戏在特定分辨率下的表现）查看所有相关硬件的性能排名。例如，在《战神：诸神黄昏》的1440p基准测试中，用户可以清晰看到AMD Radeon 9060 XT与NVIDIA RTX 5060在中端市场的性能高低，为选购提供直接依据。
3.  **数据深度与“源真相”**：与通常评测文章中经过简化（如几何平均数或归类汇总）的数据不同，Bench提供了测试中收集的原始、详尽数据。它旨在成为硬件爱好者获取可靠性能信息的“单一事实来源”。

**大规模测试更新与方法论革新（2026年计划）**
为确保数据库的时效性，Tom's Hardware正对CPU和GPU测试进行大规模、系统性的重测与方法论更新。
*   **GPU测试革新**：由资深GPU分析师Jeffrey Kampman主导。关键创新在于强调 **“真实游戏体验”**：
    *   **摒弃合成测试依赖**：不再仅仅使用流行的基准测试软件（如3DMark），而是基于玩家实际游玩的游戏。
    *   **剔除帧生成与超分辨率**：为了保证测试结果反映显卡原生性能，并使其在不同代际显卡间具有可比性，新测试方法将不使用DLSS、FSR等帧生成或超采样技术。
    *   **游戏阵容更新**：测试游戏库将纳入《远征33》、《Arc Raiders》等新作，覆盖不同游戏引擎和光线追踪场景，以全面评估GPU在各种现实场景下的表现。
    *   **测试范围广泛**：更新将分三阶段进行，覆盖从当前一代（如RTX 5000系列、Radeon 9000系列）回溯至数年前（如RTX 3000系列、Radeon 6000系列）的显卡，也包括Intel的独立显卡产品。
*   **CPU测试革新**：由CPU分析师Jake Roach等人领导一项“海格力斯式的”重测工程，目标是构建**跨越十年以上**的完整CPU数据集。
    *   **极致的测试一致性**：使用2至6台机器并行测试，通过克隆的“黄金系统镜像”、统一的测试硬件（甚至包括散热硅脂）来确保结果的一致性。
    *   **测试范围极广**：从AMD推土机架构、Intel第七代酷睿，一直到最新的Zen 5和Arrow Lake架构，涵盖数百款CPU。
    *   **耗时的精细流程**：每颗现代CPU需测试10-14小时，部分老旧型号甚至需要近一天。测试完成后，每个结果都需人工验证后才录入数据库。
    *   **全面的测试类别**：包括应用性能（渲染、编码、编译、数据科学等）、能效测试（Y-Cruncher、Handbrake、Prime95等）以及游戏测试。
    *   **游戏测试贴近实际**：游戏选择标准同样是“玩家真正在玩什么”，因此会采用《怪物猎人：荒野》、《毁灭战士：黑暗纪元》、《上古卷轴4：湮没重制版》等热门游戏，而放弃《奇点灰烬》这类纯基准测试软件。

**技术影响与应用场景**
Bench及其背后的方法论代表了硬件评测领域向**更标准化、更透明化、更以用户实际体验为中心**的演进。其影响和应用包括：
1.  **为消费者提供终极采购指南**：无论是组装新机还是升级配件，用户都可以基于具体软件或游戏需求，进行极其精准的硬件性能比对。
2.  **成为行业参考基准**：详尽的测试方法和庞大的历史数据，使其可能成为行业内评估硬件性能的参考点之一。
3.  **支持深度技术分析**：发烧友和专业人士可以挖掘底层数据，进行更深入的性能趋势分析或学术研究。
4.  **保障评测的长期价值**：通过持续重测和更新，解决了驱动更新、游戏换代导致的性能数据过时问题，使历史评测保有长期参考价值。

**总结**
Tom's Hardware Bench不仅仅是一个数据库，更是一个持续进化、致力于反映硬件真实性能的生态系统。它依托于付费订阅模式获得持续运营和深度测试所需的资源，从而能够超越常规评测周期，为订阅者提供无可比拟的硬件性能数据深度和广度。随着2026年CPU与GPU测试大规模更新的完成，Bench将建立起一个覆盖十年产品线、基于统一严谨方法论的性能图谱，成为硬核玩家和专业人士不可或缺的工具。Tom's Hardware Premium订阅费为年付每月3美元（或月付7美元），Bench是其中一项核心功能，订阅者还可获得独家分析、路线图等内容。

items.titleZh: 不到1000美元，轻松入手最快游戏CPU、主板及32GB内存——AMD锐龙7 9850X3D处理器、微星MAG X870主板与海盗船复仇者内存组合，今日Newegg套装优惠立省196美元。
items.descriptionZh:
近期，由于人工智能热潮推动存储需求激增，内存市场价格出现异常波动，例如32GB内存条价格一度飙升至近4000美元。在此背景下，捆绑销售成为获取高端PC硬件的一种高性价比方式。Newegg推出了一款新的组合套餐，将AMD最新的旗舰游戏处理器、高性能主板及DDR5内存打包销售，为组建高端游戏PC的用户提供了显著优惠。

该捆绑套餐核心包含三件组件：AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D CPU、MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板以及 Corsair Vengeance RGB 32GB (2x16GB) DDR5-6400内存套件。套餐原价1196.98美元，现价999.99美元，直接节省196.99美元。此外，购买者还将免费获得价值约126美元的两件赠品：一款华硕TUF Gaming M4 Air轻量化游戏鼠标和一份《Crimson Desert》角色扮演游戏。

**核心技术组件详解：**
1.  **AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器**：作为新一代“游戏最快CPU”，它采用AM5平台，拥有8核16线程。其最大创新在于延续了AMD备受推崇的3D V-Cache堆叠缓存技术，并在此基础之上提供了比前代9800X3D稍高的运行频率，从而实现了游戏性能的进一步提升。不过，这种性能提升是以更高的功耗为代价的。
2.  **MSI MAG X870 Tomahawk WiFi主板**：这是一款基于AMD最新平台的高端主板。其主要技术特性包括：支持PCIe 5.0标准，提供更高的数据传输带宽；配备四个M.2插槽，满足高速固态硬盘扩展需求；后置I/O面板提供了两个传输速率高达40Gbps的USB-C（雷电）接口。此外，主板还集成了最新的WiFi 7无线网络，并设计了无需工具的显卡快速释放开关，方便用户升级硬件。
3.  **Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400内存**：该内存套件容量为32GB（双通道），运行频率为6400 MT/s。这一频率被认为是AM5平台AMD处理器的“甜点”频率，能在性能、稳定性和价格之间取得良好平衡。值得注意的是，受全球AI产业对高带宽内存的强劲需求影响，此类DDR5内存价格在过去几个月内大幅上涨，该套件单独售价曾达437美元。而在此捆绑套餐中，其等效价格仅为241美元左右，性价比突出。

**性能分析与对比：**
根据Tom‘s Hardware的评测数据，Ryzen 7 9850X3D在游戏性能上确实超越了前代旗舰9800X3D，但优势幅度有限。它主要依靠小幅提升的核心频率来获得性能增益。对于追求极致游戏帧率的玩家而言，9850X3D是目前消费级市场的顶级选择。

**市场影响与应用场景：**
该捆绑套餐精准定位于高端游戏PC装机市场。在当前核心硬件（尤其是内存）价格居高不下的环境下，以低于1000美元的价格一次性购齐CPU、主板和内存三大核心部件，为用户节省了可观的预算。用户只需额外添加显卡、固态硬盘、电源、机箱和散热系统，即可组装出一台顶级的游戏电脑。对于计划构建新高端游戏系统或进行重大升级的玩家来说，此套餐提供了一个高效且经济的一站式解决方案，有效规避了单个组件市场价格波动的风险。

items.title: Nvidia reportedly working on RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM on a 96-bit bus, featuring 28 Gbps GDDR7 modules — RTX 5060 with cut-down GB205 GPU also planned
items.description: Nvidia is reportedly working on a new RTX 5050 with 9GB of GDDR7 VRAM, up from 8GB GDDR6 on the existing model. Moving from 20 Gbps chips to 28 Gbps chips, the memory interface is also said to be reduced to 96-bit, from 128-bit on the original. Moreover, an RTX 5060 with a cut-down GB205 die is also said to be in the works, with otherwise same specs.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The global component shortage is apparently affecting Nvidia — one of the catalysts of this very crisis — just as hard as other manufacturers, with the consumer segment taking the hit. Memory and chip constraints are leading the company to think of interesting new products — a new RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM seems to be in the works, according to leaker @Zed__Wang.New product: RTX5050 9GB GDDR7 96BitNV knows they can give you a 5050/5060 128-bit 12G with the new 3G GDDR7 dies. But no, you got a 5050 9G💀March 4, 2026According to the tweet, this new RTX 5050 will adopt 28 Gbps GDDR7 memory chips in favor of the existing 20 Gbps GDDR6 modules. The VRAM capacity will be upped from 8GB to 9GB, but the bus width will be cut down from 128-bit to just 96-bit. The current spec translates to 320 GB/s of bandwidth, and the updated spec will result in 336 GB/s bandwidth, which is a 5% increase.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCThe leaker goes on to mention that Nvidia could build 12GB variants of the RTX 5050 and 5060 with 3GB GDDR7 chips, but perhaps the company isn't concerned with value maximization like that, especially not in these times. The last time a desktop GPU from Nvidia rocked a 96-bit interface was back in 2024 with the RTX 3050, so it's actually not too distant of a memory. The rest of the specs should remain identical; the same GB207 die with 2,560 CUDA cores, built on TSMC's 5nm process, rated at 130W. Clock speed differences (if any) will become public knowledge as we near the potential release of this SKU. The RTX 5050 is one of the few GPUs that basically saw no price hikes in the past few months, only going up about $10. The only other card with the same stability was the RTX 5060. You may like Nvidia RTX 5070 mobile GPU looks set to get more VRAM despite global memory crisis Modders are slapping 32GB of VRAM on the RTX 5080, which isn't good for gamers Micron joins the 3GB GDDR7 party, introduces 36 Gbps modules for GPUs RTX5060 GB205 incomingNV has jammed AIC with 5060Ti 8G, and later realized Oh shit, no GB206 for 5060. And here comes the solution: 5060 based on GB205. Poor AICs need to make a new 5060 PCB design to house the GB205. Basically making a 5070 PCB with an 8-pin connector.March 4, 2026Speaking of which, @Zed__Wang also talked about a new RTX 5060 in the works with a cut-down version of the GB205 GPU — that's the silicon that powers the RTX 5070. Apparently, Nvidia has told AIBs to focus on the 8GB RTX 5060 Ti, which has led to a shortage of GB206 dies for RTX 5060 SKUs. Since both the RTX 5060 and 5060 Ti use the GB206, the chipmaker is now forced to switch gears.Defective GB205 dies might be repurposed to fit into new PCB designs for the RTX 5060 with an 8-pin power connector. Otherwise, the RTX 5070 has a 12V-2x6 connector and the GB205 die inside features 6,144 CUDA cores — those would be reduced to 3,840 CUDA cores for an RTX 5060. Moreover, the bus width on the GB205 is 192-bit and that would also go down, to 128-bit, in order to match an RTX 5060. items.titleZh: 据称英伟达正研发搭载9GB显存的RTX 5050显卡，采用96位宽总线与28Gbps GDDR7显存模组——同时计划推出采用精简版GB205 GPU的RTX 5060
items.descriptionZh:
根据知名硬件泄密者@Zed__Wang的最新信息，全球零部件短缺的浪潮正同样冲击着图形芯片巨头英伟达，迫使其考虑对即将发布的主流级显卡产品线进行非常规调整。此次调整主要围绕RTX 5050和RTX 5060两款型号，涉及显存配置与核心芯片的重新规划，反映了在当前供应链环境下厂商面临的独特挑战与应对策略。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
此次调整的核心在于显存子系统与GPU核心的重新配置。根据爆料，英伟达正规划一款全新的**RTX 5050 9GB**型号。其关键技术变化在于：
1.  **显存升级与位宽缩减**：该型号将弃用现有的20 Gbps GDDR6显存，转而采用更先进的**28 Gbps GDDR7显存颗粒**。然而，与容量从8GB提升至9GB相伴的是，显存位宽将从128位大幅**削减至96位**。这是一种在有限成本与物料下平衡带宽与容量的策略。
2.  **性能参数计算**：基于新配置计算，其显存带宽将达到**336 GB/s**（9GB * 28 Gbps / 8 * 96 / 1024）。作为对比，采用8GB 20 Gbps GDDR6和128位宽的传统设计，带宽约为320 GB/s。新设计实现了约5%的带宽提升，但窄位宽可能在高分辨率纹理加载时带来瓶颈。
3.  **潜在的替代方案**：泄密者指出，英伟达本可以使用新型的3GB容量GDDR7芯片，轻松构建128位宽、12GB显存的RTX 5050/5060，但公司似乎并未优先考虑这种“性价比最大化”的方案。

此外，泄密者还透露了关于**RTX 5060**的调整计划。由于GB206芯片（原计划用于RTX 5060/5060 Ti）供应紧张，且英伟达已要求合作厂商（AIB）优先生产8GB显存的RTX 5060 Ti，导致标准版RTX 5060芯片短缺。为此，英伟达可能采取一项应急方案：
*   **核心降级再利用**：将原本用于更高端**RTX 5070**的**GB205**芯片进行降级，屏蔽部分计算单元，用于制造RTX 5060。这意味着RTX 5060可能基于与RTX 5070同源的硅片。
*   **规格大幅调整**：GB205芯片原生拥有6,144个CUDA核心和192位显存位宽。为了适配RTX 5060的定位，其CUDA核心数量将**削减至3,840个**，显存位宽也将**降至128位**。同时，显卡的PCB设计和供电接口（预计采用传统的8-pin接口而非RTX 5070的12V-2x6接口）都需要重新设计。

**影响分析与应用场景**
1.  **市场定位与消费者选择**：RTX 5050 9GB的设计凸显了在显存容量与带宽之间寻求平衡的困境。对于主流1080p游戏玩家，9GB容量可能更有利于应对未来游戏纹理增长，但96位宽可能限制高画质下的性能发挥。这要求消费者根据具体游戏需求进行权衡。
2.  **供应链策略的体现**：RTX 5060可能采用GB205降级核心的方案，清晰地展示了英伟达如何通过灵活的芯片分级策略（利用不完全合格的芯片或调整产品规划）来应对特定芯片（GB206）的短缺，确保产品线不停滞。
3.  **成本与稳定性**：报道指出，RTX 5050和5060是近期少数价格保持相对稳定的显卡型号。新的配置调整，尤其是在不显著提升核心规格的情况下更换显存类型，可能旨在控制最终产品的成本波动。
4.  **历史参照**：英伟达在桌面GPU上使用96位显存接口并非首次，最近一次是2024年的RTX 3050。这表明该策略是其应对中低端市场细分和成本控制的工具包之一。

除了GPU核心，其余规格预计保持不变：RTX 5050仍将采用基于台积电5nm工艺的GB207芯片，拥有2,560个CUDA核心，功耗为130W。具体的核心频率信息需待产品发布时方能确认。

总而言之，这些泄露信息揭示了英伟达在全球组件短缺背景下，为维持主流显卡产品供应而采取的灵活、有时甚至是非常规的产品设计策略。这些调整深刻影响着最终产品的性能特征与市场定位，为主流游戏玩家和市场观察者提供了关于行业应对供应链危机的最新案例。

items.title: Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers
items.description:
Trump summoned tech leaders to the White House on Wednesday, March 4, 2026 to sign pledges committing their companies to foot the electricity bill for energy-hungry data centers.  | Photo: Getty Images	

Leaders from Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, Amazon, and xAI met with President Donald Trump today to sign a "rate payer protection pledge." It's one way they're responding to growing bipartisan concerns about electricity rates rising as tech companies and the Trump administration rush to build out a new generation of AI data centers. 
"[Tech companies] need some PR help because people think that if a data center goes in, their electricity prices are going to go up," Trump said during the event. "Some centers were rejected by communities for that and now I think it's going to be the opposite."

Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Leaders from Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, Amazon, and xAI met with President Donald Trump today to sign a “rate payer protection pledge.” It’s one way they’re responding to growing bipartisan concerns about electricity rates rising as tech companies and the Trump administration rush to build out a new generation of AI data centers.“[Tech companies] need some PR help because people think that if a data center goes in, their electricity prices are going to go up,” Trump said during the event. “Some centers were rejected by communities for that and now I think it’s going to be the opposite.”Trump signed a proclamation formally introducing the ratepayer protection pledge today during a roundtable event, following up on claims he made during his State of the Union speech last week. The proclamation says that “these companies will build, bring, or buy the new generation resources and electricity needed to satisfy their energy demands, and pay for all new power delivery infrastructure upgrades to service their data centers.”“People think that if a data center goes in, their electricity prices are going to go up.”The move comes as tech companies scramble to quell growing opposition to data centers that require tremendous amounts of electricity to train and run generative AI models. Household electricity bills rose 13 percent nationally in 2025, according to a December report from advocacy group Climate Power. And data center electricity demand could double or triple by 2028, the Department of Energy estimates.According to the proclamation, the seven companies present at the event have “accepted the terms of the Ratepayer Protection Pledge” and that “the commitments embodied therein effectuate the national policy of the United States.” It adds, however, that the companies would still need to “voluntarily negotiate” agreements with utilities and state governments.The president said that the companies will be responsible for adding capacity to the grid “where possible.” He also said they would cover the costs of upgrading existing power infrastructure to meet growing electricity demand. Trump added that the companies should negotiate separate rate structures with utilities, an attempt at ensuring they pay a fair rate for all the extra pressure a data center puts on the grid. Companies would be on the hook for these costs even if data centers don’t wind up using all of the additional electricity generated. That’s a key measure that could address fears that local communities would be left holding the tab for new power plants and transmission lines that become stranded assets if hype around AI fizzles out and data center projects fall flat.Trump said that the tech giants would “use their infrastructure to contribute back up power to local grids during times of need.” Reducing how much power a data center uses when electricity demand peaks — during a severe winter storm or heatwave, for example — is a measure that could help prevent power outages during disasters. This year’s winter storms have raised concerns about how new data centers could further stress power grids and hike up electricity prices during disasters. Texas passed a law last year giving the local grid operator the authority to cut data centers’ energy use during an emergency. The pledge itself is more vague, saying that companies would “whenever possible, make available their backup generation resources at times of scarcity.”During the event, Gwynne Shotwell, President and COO of SpaceX (which recently announced it’s merging with xAI with the idea of pursuing plans to shoot data centers into space), said that xAI would develop a 1.2 gigawatt power plant as its supercomputer’s primary power source. The company would similarly develop as much power generation for “every additional data center,” Shotwell said. xAi also plans to expand its Megapack installation to provide backup power to Memphis, Tennessee and Southaven, Mississippi. The NAACP has already threatened to sue xAI twice over pollution from temporary gas turbines it has installed in Tennessee and Mississippi to power its data centers.The pledge also includes a commitment to hire from local communities where data centers are under construction. Meta announced today that it launched a pilot program in Ohio to train fiber technicians, including some who attended today’s event.
items.author: Justine Calma
items.titleZh: 七家科技巨头签署特朗普的承诺书 防止数据中心周边电价飙升
items.descriptionZh:
近日，谷歌、Meta、微软、甲骨文、OpenAI、亚马逊及埃隆·马斯克旗下xAI等七家科技巨头的负责人与美国总统唐纳德·特朗普会面，共同签署了一项“费率支付人保护承诺”。此举旨在回应两党日益增长的担忧——随着科技公司与特朗普政府加速建设新一代人工智能数据中心，电价上涨可能加剧普通民众负担。

特朗普在签署仪式上表示：“科技公司需要一些公共关系援助，因为人们认为一旦数据中心入驻，他们的电费就会上涨。此前一些社区因此拒绝了数据中心项目，而现在我认为情况将会逆转。”他于当天的圆桌会议上正式签署公告，落实其上周在国情咨文演讲中的主张。公告明确要求签约企业“将建设、引入或购买满足其能源需求的新一代电力资源，并承担所有为服务其数据中心而升级的新电力输送基础设施费用”。

这一承诺出台的背景是，科技公司正竭力平息公众对数据中心的反对声浪。用于训练和运行生成式AI模型的数据中心耗电量巨大，已对电网构成压力。倡导组织“气候力量”去年12月的报告显示，2025年美国家庭电费平均上涨13%。能源部更估计，到2028年数据中心的电力需求可能增长一到两倍。

根据公告，七家公司已“接受费率支付人保护承诺的条款”，且“其中体现的承诺实现了美国的国家政策”。但文件同时指出，企业仍需与公用事业公司及州政府“自愿协商”具体协议。特朗普强调，企业将在“可能的情况下”负责为电网增加容量，并承担升级现有电力基础设施以满足激增需求的费用。他还要求科技公司与电力公司协商独立费率结构，以确保其为数据中心对电网造成的额外压力支付公平费用。

值得注意的是，即便数据中心最终未完全使用新增发电量，企业仍需承担相关成本。这一关键条款旨在缓解社区的忧虑——若人工智能热潮消退、数据中心项目失败，地方政府恐将为沦为“搁浅资产”的新建电厂和输电线路买单。

特朗普同时提出，科技巨头应“在需要时利用自身基础设施为当地电网提供备用电力”。在极端冬季风暴或热浪等用电高峰期间降低数据中心能耗，有助于防止灾难性停电。今年冬季的极端天气已引发对新数据中心可能加剧电网压力并推高灾害期间电价的担忧。得克萨斯州去年通过的法律已授权当地电网运营商在紧急情况下削减数据中心用电。但本次承诺的表述较为模糊，仅称企业将“尽可能在电力短缺时提供备用发电资源”。

SpaceX总裁兼首席运营官格温·肖特维尔（该公司近期宣布将与xAI合并，探索将数据中心发射至太空的计划）在会上透露，xAI将建设一座1.2吉瓦的发电厂作为其超级计算机的主要电源，并承诺为“每个新增数据中心”配套建设相应规模的发电设施。xAI还计划扩展其Megapack储能装置，为田纳西州孟菲斯和密西西比州南黑文提供备用电力。此前，全美有色人种协进会已因xAI在田纳西和密西西比使用临时燃气轮机为数据中心供电造成的污染问题，两度威胁对其提起诉讼。

该承诺还包括在数据中心建设当地招聘员工的条款。Meta当日宣布在俄亥俄州启动光纤技术人员培训试点项目，部分参与培训者出席了本次签署仪式。这项涵盖能源责任、基础设施投资与社区就业的多维度承诺，折射出人工智能爆发式增长背后亟待平衡的技术野心、能源现实与社会成本。

items.title: Taiwan expects power demand to increase by more than 5GW by 2030, enough to power nearly 4 million homes — rise in electricity consumption driven by semiconductor manufacturing and AI data center deployments
items.description: New semiconductor fabs and data centers are fueling Taiwan's growing power demand, with the island expected to require more than 5GW of new electricity sources and power infrastructure to keep up.
